title: Phyllanthus scopulorum
intro: Phyllanthus scopulorum, also known as Scopulophyllanthus scopulorum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and preventing the progression of viral infections. The bioactive constituents of Phyllanthus scopulorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Phyllanthus scopulorum is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to treat various health conditions, including fever, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Phyllanthus scopulorum is classified within the family Phyllanthaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as the Phyllanthus or leaf-flower family. This family is part of the order Malpighiales, which consists of various flowering plants. The order Malpighiales is further classified within the clade rosids. The clade rosids, in turn, is part of the larger clade eurosids II. Phyllanthus scopulorum also belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, which encompasses flowering plants with a specific type of flower structure.
